mnemonics: CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability - The three pillars of database security in the cloud, just like the Central Intelligence Agency protects national secrets.
keyPoints: The Shared Responsibility Model defines security 'of' the cloud (provider) vs. security 'in' the cloud (customer).
Data encryption is crucial both at rest (storage) and in transit (over the network).
Identity and Access Management (IAM) is the primary mechanism for controlling who can access what data.
commonMistakes: Assuming the cloud provider is fully responsible for securing your data (neglecting customer responsibilities).
Granting excessive permissions to users or services (violating the principle of least privilege).
